name: Pottawattamie County v. McGhee
question: May a prosecutor be subject to civil prosecution when he allegedly violated the criminal defendants' substantial due process rights by fabricating evidence and then introducing it at trial against the defendants?
description: A case which the Court dismissed before argument concerning whether or not a prosecutor can be subject to civil prosecution for allegedly violating a criminal defendants' substantial due process rights by fabricating evidence and then introducing that evidence at trial.
